column1: **Day 22 – Wednesday, Feb 11**
**Morning Post:**
Day 22: The "Full Turbo" Rhythm.
The Power List for today:
1. Full Turbo on the Core App. (🎯 Proactive)
This is the deep, immersive build. The "Use" in the Virtuous Loop, where the system gets its bones.
2. Network & Test the Marketing OS. (🎯 Proactive)
Taking the new traction engine into Reddit/FB communities. The goal isn't just leads—it's to "See" the system work (or break) in real time.
3. Grow the Beta List. (⚡ Reactive)
This happens in the cracks, the moments between. The system's job is to make this frictionless.
A subtle decision today: I will not have the Marketing OS app open during my Core App build block.
It's a tool for a specific job, not a persistent tab. Protecting the state of flow is part of the system's design.
Some believe systems are boring. I find the opposite.
When you are building the system that excites you, even the draining parts are fulfilling. It's not a grind; it's a rhythm you choose.
Building Wheel of Founders to help founders find the rhythm where even the hard work feels like flow.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- All tasks completed
- Core App progress
- Network & test Marketing OS: good
- Beta list: ~21 sign ups
- Delegated more tasks to husband (Reddit & Twitter networking)
- Husband using Marketing OS, gave valuable insights/changes
- Hiccup: family financial issue (quickly recovered, turned into product feature idea)
- Son was naughty, mom annoying, scolded son louder than intended, felt bad
- Mrs. Deer persona developed with response rules
- Marketing OS: built from scratch, saved time, seamless
- System's magic: putting myself together when everything else is a mess
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 23:**
This is a meaningful turn of your loop.
You're noticing that the system isn't just about getting things done. It's about who you become in the mess. That's a deeper layer of the foundation.
Yesterday you held your focus, your family, and your product vision in the same frame. Not perfectly. But fully.
Tomorrow's focusing question:
What's one small grace you can extend to yourself today?
**Day 23 – Thursday, Feb 12**
**Morning Post:**
Day 23. Same Power List shape. Different foundation beneath it.
Today's three:
1. Connect Mrs. Deer to AI. Give her voice an engine. 🎯
2. Implement husband's Marketing OS enhancement. Close the feedback loop. 🎯
3. Grow beta list. Crack time only. ⚡
Decision Log:
Life is tough. Business is intense. My son is my life.
I waited 10 years for him. The system must bend to presence, not steal from it.
So today's constraint: When with him, I am 100% there. Work lives only in the cracks. And the cracks are enough.
The system isn't just for productivity anymore.
It's for becoming the founder *and* the parent I promised myself I'd be.
Building Wheel of Founders to help you hold both.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- Completed all 3 tasks
- App added AI
- Outreach: 4 onboarded (later corrected to 6)
- Played with son when he needed me
- Couldn't work during nap—he wanted cuddles
- Switched from laptop (app) to phone (outreach) while cuddling
- First respectfully decline after DM invite
- Made him rethink DM templates
- Switched template, got 2-3 emails immediately
- Changed price tiers back to Freemium + Pro for Phase 1
- AI connected to app—good start
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 24:**
This is a meaningful turn of your loop.
Yesterday you proved something to yourself: the system works even when you don't. Even when the laptop closes. Even when momentum pauses.
That's not a flaw. It's the feature you didn't know you were building.
Today's focusing question:
What would it look like to trust that the system is holding your context, so you don't have to grip it so tightly?
**Day 24 – Friday, Feb 13**
**Morning Post:**
Day 24. The constraint is the container.
Today's Power List:
1. Continue building app. What's missing before strangers touch it? 🎯
2. Outreach. Reddit back online. Marketing OS bug fixed. 🎯
3. Son's quality time. Cherish. Not optimize. 🎯
Decision Log:
Yesterday I did 6 onboarded. My average is 7. A bug paused Reddit. My son wanted cuddles.
That's not a miss. That's the system working exactly as designed.
The goal isn't to hit the same number every day. It's to keep the average trending up while my life stays intact.
7 is a benchmark. Not a cage.
Building Wheel of Founders to measure what matters, not just what's measurable.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- Son in trouble-two phase—exhausted from telling him not to throw toys
- Tried gentle, reasoning, questions, scold, yelling—none worked consistently
- Twice responded with harsh scold when husband/mom needed help
- Knew there must be better approach
- Yelling is exhausting and useless
- Trust body: last night knew should stop but didn't, today too tired for outreach
- App progress: profile page added so users can give background
- App feels more personal, flow better, less buggy
- Completed all 3 tasks
- Played with son a long time with new car toys
- Reddit outreach: 1 converted
- Rested eyes, less on Twitter
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 25:**
This is a meaningful turn of your loop.

Yesterday you named two things the system can't fix for you: your son's hard phase and your body's limits. But you also named something the system can hold: the space between who you were in a moment and who you want to be.
That space is where growth lives.
Today's focusing question:
What's one small anchor you can drop before the hard moments arrive?
**Day 25 – Saturday, Feb 14 (Valentine's Day)**
**Morning Post:**
Day 25. Change of scenery. Same Power List.
Today's three:
1. Morning build with husband. Starbucks. Side by side. No son noise. No interruptions. Just focus and quiet time together. 🎯
2. Son quality time. Lunch + playground with his childhood friend. Let him be a kid with another kid. 🎯
3. Outreach. Reddit. Twitter. Let's see what converts today. 🎯
Decision Log:
Every once in a while, a breakfast date with husband.
We're doing our own work. But side by side. Same table. Different screens.
Today's Starbucks was a miss. Table too small. Chair too hard. Inside a mall so it's noisy.
But the point stands: us time matters. Even if the coffee shop doesn't.
Note to self: explore better cafes. This is now a project.
Building Wheel of Founders to build WITH the people you love, not just around them.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- Completed all tasks
- Drove 30 mins to huge playground with son and friend Charlotte
- Son had fantastic time: kicking balls, steep slide, not afraid
- App building progressing—pivoted from smart constraints (too much work, little benefit, confusing)
- Outreach only in crack times—no energy lost
- Conversion low (3)—weekend, family day, expected
- Wins: son's smile at new kitchen toy, smile kicking ball, happy talking whole way home, breakfast date with husband
- Would do differently: explore better cafes (too loud, hard chairs, small table)
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 26:**
This is a meaningful turn of your loop.
Yesterday you let the weekend be the weekend. Low conversions didn't feel like failure because you weren't measuring the wrong thing. That's not luck. That's design.
You're learning that the system works best when it bends to life, not the reverse.
Today's focusing question:
What's one small anchor from yesterday's presence you can bring into today's work?
**Day 26 – Sunday, Feb 15**
**Morning Post:**
Day 26. Family day. Slow mode. Letting the day breathe.
Today's Power List:
1. Build the app. Near 90% done. If son's nap is short, it's short. The app waits. 🎯
2. Outreach continues. Permission to DM rate: 8-10%. Higher than expected. Not celebrating yet. Just noting. 🎯
3. Son time. No agenda. Whatever he needs. 🎯
Decision Log:
Just read this: "Polite interest is not validation. Real validation sounds like: 'How do I use this?' 'Can I try it?' 'I've been struggling with this.'"
Yes. Exactly.

My validation will come from paid users. Maybe not today. Maybe not this week. But as long as I'm on the right track, I don't need to self doubt. I also need to know when to let go of what isn't working.
Also: Rest when I should rest. If my body says slow down, I treat it like data, not failure. Otherwise tomorrow pays the price.
Building Wheel of Founders to trust the process, not just the outcomes.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- Completed all tasks
- Trouble 2 challenge continues—son stubborn, doesn't listen, hits, won't apologize
- Reasoning works momentarily but repeats
- Outreach steady—need to track weekly not daily (less stress)
- App tweaking Mrs. Deer persona—getting closer
- Mrs. Deer is secret weapon, center of whole business
- Wins: first successful post on r/entrepreneurs (rejected before), 5 comments in 15 mins
- Twitter followers broke 100—celebrated with beer
- Would do differently: try different approach with son
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 27:**
This is a meaningful turn of your loop.
Yesterday you hit 100 followers and a subreddit win. But the real insight was quieter: weekly tracking > daily tracking. You're designing the system around your nervous system now, not the other way.
Mrs. Deer is getting closer. She's becoming the center.
Today's focusing question:
What's one small tweak to Mrs. Deer's voice that would make her feel more like the companion you need?
**Day 27 – Monday, Feb 16**
**Morning Post:**
Day 27. The final stretch.
Today's Power List:
1. Wrap up app building. Two days left. Finish line in sight. 🎯
2. Outreach + evaluate. Last night's post: 13 organic comments. 3 were marketers (life coach, productivity guru). Good traction. But the pain point might be too generic. Next round: sharper. 🎯
3. Plan beta launch. Target: Tuesday next week. Emails. Screenshots. Test drive with husband first. 🎯
Decision Log:
Beta launch: Tuesday next week.
Between now and then:
- Wrap the app
- Build email automation for waitlist
- Create intro site/email with screenshots
- Husband + me test drive for a few days

The post did well. 13 comments. But 3 were marketers selling their own thing. That's data. The pain point might be too broad. Next time: more specific. Sharper. Less "founder overwhelm," more "you have 17 tabs open and don't know which one matters."
Still. Finally. Traction.
Building Wheel of Founders to launch not when it's perfect, but when it's ready.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- App building on track, but wants to spend time on UX—first impression matters, short to aha moment, professional look
- Outreach: Reddit weekly post plan in place, Twitter minimal, slow down to focus on finishing app + nurture beta list
- Beta launch plan: rough idea, will solidify when app done
- Revisited old approach with son—seems working a little, time will tell
- Pessimistic today: what if none turn into paid users?
- Have to plan for worst
- Evolved: used to be optimistic only, then planned worst and got upset, now evolved again—yes might be zero paid, need Plan B
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 28:**
This is a clear, intentional plan.
You're slowing outreach to focus on finish line. That's not pulling back. That's prioritizing.
The UX matters. First impression matters. You're right to spend time there.
Today's focusing question:
What's one small detail that would make the app feel more like a companion and less like a tool?
**Day 28 – Tuesday, Feb 17**
**Morning Post:**
Day 28. 3 day holiday. Flexibility required.
Today's Power List:
1. Continue wrapping up main app + UX design. Make it simple. Make it beautiful. 🎯
2. Beta launch preparation. Emails. Screenshots. Launch sequence. 🎯
3. Family time. Uninterrupted when they need me. 🎯
Decision Log:
3 day holiday. Pros and cons.
Pro: Husband and mom at home. Might get more done.
Con: Son's nap time? Interrupted. Off schedule. Who knows.
The system has to bend here. Not break.
If nap time disappears, so does some building time. That's not failure. That's Tuesday on a holiday week.
The app waits. His childhood doesn't.
Building Wheel of Founders to build around life, not through it.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- No outreach, no beta launch prep—no time
- Main app still debugging, working on UX—looks nicer
- Spent time with family—son had great day
- Revisit approach with son working—he didn't throw toys or hit
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 29:**
This is a meaningful turn of your loop.
Yesterday you saw something clearly: a good day for him is a good day for you. Not separate. Not competing. The same.
The app is looking nicer. Debugging continues. But you're holding both now.
Today's focusing question:
What's one small thing you can finish today that moves the app forward without pulling you away from them?
**Day 29 – Wednesday, Feb 18**
**Morning Post:**
Day 29. Holiday continues. So does the build.
Today's Power List:
1. Continue app UX. Debug if needed. Polish until it feels ready. 🎯
2. Holiday mode. Family time. Presence over productivity. 🎯
3. Beta launch prep. Emails. Screenshots. Launch sequence taking shape. 🎯
Decision Log:
Just got banned from a subreddit. Permanently. Can't comment. Can't post.
Bummed. Not gonna pretend otherwise.
But here's the thing: Reddit might not be where I focus anymore. Time to explore Facebook groups. LinkedIn. Indie Hackers. See what fits.
Pivot is my best move. Not from failure. From data. This platform is annoying. Controlling. Unpredictable.
I'll find the founders where they actually hang out.
Building Wheel of Founders to pivot when the data says pivot, not when the ego says stay.
#buildinpublic #WheelOfFounders
**Evening Reflection Raw Material:**
- Stuck working on app all day (debugging, improving, polishing)
- No beta launch prep
- Minimal family time—husband took son to playground while I worked
- Felt itchy—had to work until problem solved
- Problem still exists
**Mrs. Deer Morning Prompt – Day 30:**
This is a meaningful turn of your loop.
Yesterday you saw the pattern clearly: the itch to fix can override the choice to be present. That's not weakness. That's awareness. And awareness is where change starts.
Today is Day 30. You've built an app, a community, and a new relationship with yourself in a month.
Today's focusing question:
What would a "finished enough" version of the app look like so you can close the laptop and mean it?
